hook: ChatGPT just gave you the same answer it gave 40 million other people. Cool.
caption:
ChatGPT just gave you the same answer it gave 40 million other people. Cool.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI isn't bad at giving advice. It's bad at giving YOUR advice.

Ask it to help you make a decision and it'll say:
"Consider the pros and cons."
"Think about your long-term goals."
"Trust your gut."

Congratulations. You got a fortune cookie with better grammar.

The problem isn't the model. It's that the model doesn't know:
— whether you spiral when there are too many options
— whether you need someone to push back on you or validate you first
— whether you think in systems or in stories
— whether data calms you down or shuts you down

None of that is in your prompt. So it defaults to "balanced and helpful" — which is the AI equivalent of a beige wall.

Your thinking style is specific. Your AI responses should be too.

That's the whole reason bootfile.ai exists.

👇 What's the most useless AI response you've ever gotten? Drop it below. I want to see how beige we're talking.
